widgets_values:
# VRGDG LTX LoRA Training Workflow

## What This Workflow Does

This workflow trains an LTX LoRA in chunks, creates preview videos after each saved chunk, and then builds a final XYZ comparison video when training reaches the final target step.

## Main Nodes

### `VRGDG_LTXLoraTrainChunk`
This is the main training node.

It handles:
- dataset folder prep
- optional caption creation
- cache creation and reuse
- musubi config generation
- LoRA training resume from latest state
- loading the newest trained LoRA back onto the `MODEL` output
- generating a safe `video_filename_prefix` for downstream preview videos

### `VRGDG_LTXPreviewXYZPlot`
This node creates a final comparison video from all saved preview videos.

It only runs when:
- `completed_steps >= total_target_steps`

It automatically:
- finds the preview videos for the current run
- figures out a square-ish grid layout
- labels each tile from the preview filename
- creates a new output filename if a prior XYZ video already exists

## Training Node Setup

### Dataset Folder
`dataset_images_dir` can be anywhere.

The node supports:
- pointing directly to an `images` folder
- or pointing to a parent dataset folder

If needed, it will organize images into an `images` subfolder.

Supported image types:
- `.png`
- `.jpg`
- `.jpeg`
- `.webp`
- `.bmp`
- `.gif`
- `.tif`
- `.tiff`

### Workspace Folder
`workspace_dir` can also be anywhere.

If it does not exist, the node creates it.

Inside that folder, the node creates:
- `cache`
- `output`
- `logs`
- `config`

## Important Training Behavior

### Resume
Each new run resumes from the latest saved state folder in:
- `workspace_dir/output`

### Save Behavior
`steps_per_run` is also the save interval.

That means:
- each run trains one chunk
- each run saves at the end of that chunk
- each run can immediately generate a preview video downstream

### Cache Reuse
If cache already exists and cache mode is set to auto:
- the node reuses cache
- it skips rebuilding cache

This is useful when retesting the workflow without waiting for cache again.

### Memory Cleanup
The node can clear Comfy memory before the Gemma text-encoder stage.

This helps reduce stalls during:
- text encoder caching
- heavy model loading

## Outputs Used Downstream

The training node outputs:
- `model`
- `latest_state_path`
- `log_path`
- `video_filename_prefix`
- `output_name`
- `completed_steps`
- `total_target_steps`

Most important downstream connections:
- `model` -> preview generation branch
- `video_filename_prefix` -> VHS `Combine Video` `filename_prefix`
- `output_name` -> `VRGDG_LTXPreviewXYZPlot`
- `completed_steps` -> `VRGDG_LTXPreviewXYZPlot`
- `total_target_steps` -> `VRGDG_LTXPreviewXYZPlot`

## Preview Video Naming

The training node outputs a VHS-safe prefix like:

`datasetname_YYYY-MM-DD_HH-MM-SS/LTXChunkRun_step_250`

This means:
- all preview videos for one run go into the same dated folder
- each preview video includes the training step in its filename

That step number is what the XYZ node uses for tile labels.

## XYZ Plot Node Behavior

### When It Runs
It only creates the comparison video when training is finished:
- `completed_steps >= total_target_steps`

If not final yet:
- it does nothing

### Layout
The node now auto-calculates columns based on the number of preview videos.

Examples:
- `4 videos` -> `2 columns`
- `6 videos` -> `3 columns`
- `9 videos` -> `3 columns`

### Tile Size
If:
- `cell_width = 0`
- `cell_height = 0`

the node auto-detects preview resolution from the first video.

### Labels
If `label_tiles = true`:
- each tile gets a label above it
- the label is taken from the preview video filename

### Existing XYZ Outputs
If an XYZ compare video already exists:
- the node creates a new file with a suffix like `_v2`, `_v3`

It also ignores older `_XYZ_COMPARE_` files when gathering source previews.

## Render Backend

The XYZ node supports:
- `CPU (libx264)`
- `NVIDIA GPU (h264_nvenc)`

GPU mode speeds up encoding when NVENC is available.

## TensorBoard

The training config logs to TensorBoard.

You can use the `Show TensorBoard` button on the training node to open the training logs in a browser.

TensorBoard is useful for viewing:
- loss curves
- step progress
- training metrics over time

## Recommended Basic Flow

1. Set `dataset_images_dir`
2. Set `workspace_dir`
3. Set training values like `steps_per_run`, `total_target_steps`, `learning_rate`, and `blocks_to_swap`
4. Connect `model` output from trainer into your preview generation branch
5. Connect `video_filename_prefix` into VHS `Combine Video`
6. Connect `output_name`, `completed_steps`, and `total_target_steps` into `VRGDG_LTXPreviewXYZPlot`
7. Let the workflow loop until final step is reached
8. The XYZ node creates the final comparison video automatically at the end

You will need to install this first

[AkaneTendo25/musubi-tuner](https://github.com/AkaneTendo25/musubi-tuner/tree/ltx-2-dev)

nodes.title: LTX models used in the video creation part of the workflow.
widgets_values:
## 📦 Required Models & Assets

### 🧠 Diffusion Model
- **ltx-2.3-distilled-Q6_k.gguf**  
  🔗 [Download](https://huggingface.co/QuantStack/LTX-2.3-GGUF/tree/main/LTX-2.3-distilled)

---

### 🎥 Video & 🔊 Audio VAE
- **LTX2.3_video_vae_bf16.safetensors**  
- **LTX2.3_audio_vae_bf16.safetensors**  
  🔗 [Download](https://huggingface.co/Kijai/LTX2.3_comfy/tree/main/vae)

---

### 📝Clip Text / Instruction Model
- **gemma-3-12b-it-qat-q4_0-gguf**  
  🔗 [Download](https://huggingface.co/google/gemma-3-12b-it-qat-q4_0-gguf/tree/main)

---

### 🔤 Clip Text Encoder / Embeddings
- **ltx-2.3_text_projection_bf16.safetensors**  
  🔗 [Download](https://huggingface.co/Kijai/LTX2.3_comfy/tree/main/text_encoders)

---

### 🔍 Spatial Upscaler
- **ltx-2.3-spatial-upscaler-x2-1.0.safetensors**  
  🔗 [Download](https://huggingface.co/Lightricks/LTX-2.3/blob/main/ltx-2.3-spatial-upscaler-x2-1.0.safetensors)

---

```
📂 ComfyUI/
├── 📂 models/
│   ├── 📂 diffusion_models/
│   │      └── ltx-2.3-distilled-Q6_k.gguf
│   ├── 📂 text_encoders/
│   │      ├── ltx-2.3-text_projection_bf16.safetensors
│   │      └── gemma-3-12b-it-q4_0.gguf
│   ├── 📂 vae/
│   │      ├── LTX2.3_video_vae_bf16.safetensors
│   │      └── LTX2.3_audio_vae_bf16.safetensors
│   └── 📂 latent_upscale_models/
│          └── ltx-2.3-spatial-upscaler-x2-1.0.safetensors

```

nodes.title: Positive Prompt
widgets_values:
31v3nR3a1ist1c 

A female elf stands in a wide sunlit meadow filled with tall green grass and scattered wildflowers. The meadow stretches into the distance with rolling hills and clusters of trees along the horizon. Warm afternoon sunlight pours across the field from the upper left side of the sky, creating bright highlights across the grass and soft golden haze in the air. Butterflies flutter throughout the scene, moving between flowers and drifting through the sunlight.

The elf stands in the foreground about two meters from the camera. She has elegant pointed ears visible through her long flowing hair, which moves gently in the breeze. Resting on her head is a delicate jeweled tiara with small reflective gemstones that sparkle in the sunlight. She wears a flowing lace-trimmed gown with intricate fabric textures that move softly with the wind. The overall aesthetic is 31v3nR3a1ist1c, highly detailed and natural with soft cinematic lighting.

The camera begins in a medium close-up framing from waist level, positioned slightly to her right side. Tall grass sways slowly around her legs while butterflies pass through the foreground and background at different depths.

The camera slowly pushes forward while drifting from right to left, creating gentle parallax between the elf in the foreground and the rolling meadow behind her. Several butterflies flutter past the lens and circle around her shoulders as sunlight glints off the jewels in her tiara.

She initially looks out across the meadow toward the horizon, then slowly turns her head toward the camera. As the camera reaches a close portrait framing, she meets the viewer’s gaze and softly says with a warm curious tone, “Well, how do I look?”

Her hair and gown continue moving lightly in the breeze while butterflies drift through the sunlit air behind her and the camera finishes its slow push into a close-up of her face
